# pocketid_ldap_test (Data Source)

Tests the current LDAP configuration. This data source attempts to connect to the configured LDAP server and validate the settings.

-> **Note** This data source reflects the current LDAP configuration in Pocket-ID. It's useful for validating configuration before enabling synchronization.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Test the current LDAP configuration
data "pocketid_ldap_test" "test" {}

# Display test results
output "ldap_connection_status" {
  value = data.pocketid_ldap_test.test.connection_successful ? "Connected" : "Failed"
}

output "ldap_test_details" {
  value = data.pocketid_ldap_test.test.connection_successful ? "LDAP connection successful" : data.pocketid_ldap_test.test.error_message
}

# Use test results to conditionally enable LDAP
resource "pocketid_ldap_config" "conditional" {
  # Only enable if we can connect
  enabled = data.pocketid_ldap_test.test.connection_successful

  url           = "ldap://ldap.example.com:389"
  bind_dn       = "cn=admin,dc=example,dc=com"
  bind_password = var.ldap_password
  base_dn       = "dc=example,dc=com"

  user_attributes {
    unique_identifier = "uid"
    username          = "uid"
  }
}

# Validate configuration meets requirements
data "pocketid_ldap_test" "validation" {}

locals {
  ldap_ready = alltrue([
    data.pocketid_ldap_test.validation.connection_successful,
    data.pocketid_ldap_test.validation.bind_successful,
    data.pocketid_ldap_test.validation.base_dn_found,
    data.pocketid_ldap_test.validation.users_found > 0
  ])
}

resource "pocketid_ldap_sync" "sync" {
  # Only sync if LDAP is properly configured
  count = local.ldap_ready ? 1 : 0

  triggers = {
    ready = local.ldap_ready
  }
}

# Monitor LDAP health
data "pocketid_ldap_test" "health_check" {}

output "ldap_health" {
  value = {
    connected    = data.pocketid_ldap_test.health_check.connection_successful
    authenticated = data.pocketid_ldap_test.health_check.bind_successful
    base_dn_valid = data.pocketid_ldap_test.health_check.base_dn_found
    users_found   = data.pocketid_ldap_test.health_check.users_found
    groups_found  = data.pocketid_ldap_test.health_check.groups_found
  }
}

# Alert on LDAP issues
output "ldap_warnings" {
  value = data.pocketid_ldap_test.health_check.connection_successful ? null : "WARNING: LDAP connection failed - ${data.pocketid_ldap_test.health_check.error_message}"
}

# Validate before making changes
data "pocketid_ldap_test" "pre_change" {}

resource "null_resource" "ldap_validation" {
  lifecycle {
    precondition {
      condition     = data.pocketid_ldap_test.pre_change.connection_successful
      error_message = "LDAP must be properly configured before proceeding: ${data.pocketid_ldap_test.pre_change.error_message}"
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Read-Only

- `base_dn_found` (Boolean) Whether the base DN was found and accessible.
- `bind_successful` (Boolean) Whether LDAP bind authentication was successful.
- `connection_successful` (Boolean) Whether the connection to the LDAP server was successful.
- `error_message` (String) Error message if any test failed.
- `groups_found` (Number) Number of groups found in LDAP directory.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier for this test (always 'ldap-test').
- `test_timestamp` (String) When this test was performed.
- `users_found` (Number) Number of users found in LDAP directory.

## Notes

* This data source performs a read-only test of the LDAP configuration
* No changes are made to either Pocket-ID or the LDAP server
* The test includes:
  - Network connectivity to the LDAP server
  - TLS certificate validation (if using LDAPS)
  - Bind authentication with the configured credentials
  - Base DN accessibility
  - User and group search filter validation
* Results are computed at the time of the Terraform plan/apply
